surface_symptom: The deal.II linear_elasticity catalog entry described the physics in prose (function_space string, pitfall strings) but did not enumerate the relevant FE classes or GridGenerator functions under structured `elements` / `mesh_generators` keys. The Layer A→B catalog-vs-scan diff (2026-05-31) surfaced 26 truly-missing element classes and 32 truly-missing mesh generators for deal.II overall, and 7 entries (FE_DGP, FE_DGQ, FE_Q, FE_RaviartThomas, hyper_cube, hyper_rectangle, subdivided_hyper_rectangle) that the catalog *mentioned* in pitfall prose but did not lift into a structured key — meaning the agent calling `prepare_simulation(solver='dealii', physics='linear_elasticity')` saw scattered references rather than a clean element/mesh menu. Worst-case consequence: the agent picks FE_Q(1) for a nearly-incompressible problem and hits volumetric locking silently, because FE_RannacherTurek and FE_Q_Bubbles are nowhere mentioned.
root_cause: The deal.II catalog was written one-physics-at-a-time from the corresponding step-* tutorial, so each entry mentioned only the FE class that tutorial happened to use (FE_Q for step-8). The structured-element-menu pattern that Kratos's catalog uses for `linear_elasticity.elements.{2D,3D,shells,beams}` was never applied to deal.II — every deal.II physics has only `function_space` (a single string) and `pitfalls`. The catalog-vs-scan diff is the first cross-cutting check that surfaces this asymmetry.
pitfall_db_entries: [Numerical] FE_Q<dim>(1) (linear elements) locks in nearly-incompressible elasticity (Poisson ratio nu close to 0.5). Use FE_Q<dim>(2) (quadratic), FE_RannacherTurek<dim>() (P1-NC), or a mixed displacement-pressure formulation with FE_Q+FE_DGQ for pressure. Signal: tip deflection on a compressible beam converges with refinement, but converges to the wrong value as nu → 0.5 (volumetric locking).

[API] FE_Nothing<dim>() inside an FESystem on a subdomain where displacement should be inactive does NOT skip assembly on those cells — it just makes the DoF count zero there. You still need to mark the cells with a manifold ID or a hp::DoFHandler<dim> active_fe_index switch. Signal: assembly enters cells you thought were 'off' and accumulates garbage into the rhs.

[Numerical] Use SolverCG only when the stiffness matrix is symmetric positive-definite. Adding a Dirichlet penalty (rather than constraining the DoFs) keeps it SPD; using asymmetric face stabilisation or a Nitsche-style boundary term breaks symmetry — switch to SolverGMRES. Signal: SolverCG reports 'breakdown' or stalls at 1e-2 residual reduction.
agent_detection_after_fix: The agent now retrieves a structured `elements` menu via prepare_simulation(solver='dealii', physics='linear_elasticity') listing FE_Q with degree advice + alternative elements (FE_Q_Bubbles, FE_Q_Hierarchical, FE_RannacherTurek, FE_Nothing) each with a one-line physics-relevant rationale; a structured `mesh_generators` menu listing 9 GridGenerator functions; and structured `solvers` + `preconditioners` menus. Pre-execution critic gate retrieves these and the new pitfalls (locking, FE_Nothing semantics, SolverCG SPD requirement) when the agent's plan touches deal.II elasticity. Catalog-vs-scan diff coverage_gap for the elements bucket drops from 30 → ~22 after this commit (the 8 newly-listed elements + the 3 already-uncategorised FE_Q/FE_DGP/FE_DGQ).
source_refs: Open-FEM-Agent §3.2 / Table 1 (Syntax / Physics / Numerical / API / Integration)
task #33 — Encode discovered upstream capabilities into MCP catalog
task #46 — Pitfall-DB-first merge discipline (every fix ships pitfall entry + post-mortem)
deal.II step-8 (basic LE), step-18 (quasi-static), and the upstream `fe_rannacher_turek.h` for the locking-free choice
